title: gnetum thoa
intro: Gnetum thoa, also known as Vietnamese coriander or Gai luc, is a type of plant in the family Gnetaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as the "living fossils." Traditionally, Gnetum thoa has been used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and improving digestion, as well as having antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Gnetum thoa is often used in tea, capsule, or tincture form, and it is also used as a spice in cooking, particularly in Vietnamese and Thai cuisine.
